plan_review_notes: Approve the entrypoint/config slice: implement sisyphus module wrappers and config dual-read, keep taskflow compatibility shims, and leave protocol/persisted identifiers out of scope.

plan_review_history.notes: Revised the task to the concrete migration slice: add canonical sisyphus CLI/MCP module entrypoints, move canonical console scripts to those modules, add .sisyphus.toml preferred config loading with .taskflow.toml fallback, and update docs/tests accordingly while preserving taskflow compatibility.
Approve the entrypoint/config slice: implement sisyphus module wrappers and config dual-read, keep taskflow compatibility shims, and leave protocol/persisted identifiers out of scope.

spec_review_notes: Freeze scope to canonical sisyphus CLI/MCP module entrypoints, pyproject script routing, .sisyphus.toml preferred config/discovery with legacy fallback, and matching docs/tests. Do not rename src/taskflow, taskflow.event.v1, or TF task ids in this task.

normal_cases.name: Canonical `sisyphus` CLI and MCP module entrypoints resolve to working wrappers.
A repository configured only with `.sisyphus.toml` loads expected settings and is discoverable as a repo root.

edge_cases.name: When both config files are present, `.sisyphus.toml` wins deterministically.
A repository that still has only `.taskflow.toml` continues to work without migration.

exception_cases.name: This slice does not accidentally rename high-risk protocol or persisted identifiers such as `taskflow.event.v1` or `TF-...`.
Direct launcher docs do not point to nonexistent module paths.

verification_methods.target: Canonical sisyphus CLI and MCP module entrypoints resolve to working wrappers.
A repository configured only with .sisyphus.toml loads expected settings and is discoverable as a repo root.
Config precedence and legacy fallback remain deterministic.
High-risk identifiers are unchanged and docs point to real module paths.
